'''Gary Broadbent''' (born 31 October 1976), also known as '''"Broady",''' is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. 

He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[RFL Championship|National League One]], as a {{rlp|fb|ce}} or {{rlp|so}}.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/news/broadbent_puts_retirement_decision_on_hold_1_593612?referrerPath=home|title=Broadbent puts retirement decision on hold|date=4 August 2009|publisher=North West Evening Mail|accessdate=2009-08-14|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605103531/http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/news/broadbent_puts_retirement_decision_on_hold_1_593612?referrerPath=home|archivedate=5 June 2011}}</ref>

==Background==
Broadbent was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria]], England and raised on Walney Island.

==Club career==
Broadbent left the [[Widnes Vikings]] for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 1997 for a fee of £50,000 (based on increases in average earnings, this would be approximately £86,080 in 2013).<ref name="Measuring Worth - Relative Value of UK Pounds">{{cite web|url=http://www.measuringworth.com/ukcompare|title=Measuring Worth - Relative Value of UK Pounds|publisher=Measuring Worth|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015}}</ref> 

He then joined [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in 2003. In 2008, he became the club record holder for most consecutive appearances, breaking the previous record set by [[David Fatialofa]]. He went on to make 83 appearances without missing a game for the club.<ref>{{cite web|title=Club Records|url=http://www.whitehavenrl.co.uk/page.php?id=1748|publisher=Whitehaven RLFC|accessdate=24 May 2014|archive-date=14 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140314203939/http://www.whitehavenrl.co.uk/page.php?id=1748|url-status=dead}}</ref> 

He finished his career with hometown club [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]], playing his last game in September 2011 against [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Gary Broadbent bows out after 18 years of rugby league|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14902460|work=BBC Sport|access-date=24 May 2014|date=14 September 2011}}</ref>

==International honours==
Gary Broadbent won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|Emerging England]] while at Salford in 1998 in a 15–12 victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Elder statesmen negotiate England escape|newspaper=The Times|date=20 July 1998|page=32|publisher=News International Trading Limited|location=London|id={{ProQuest|317976921}}}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Broadbent is employed by [[BNFL]] at [[Sellafield]], and currently resides in Barrow-in-Furness with his wife, Emma and together they have two children, Sophie (born 1999) and Luke (born 2002).
